#  Edit Drawing Markups 
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * _To edit any markups on both the 'Personal' and 'Published' layers,_ 'Admin' permissions on the project's Drawings tool.  
_Note_ : 'Standard' users can only edit personal and published markups that they have created.
    * Published markups can only be deleted by users with ‘Admin’ permissions on the Drawings tool, unless a user is [granted specific permissions to delete published markups](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/allow-users-to-delete-published-markups).
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Any changes made to the markups will automatically be updated on the drawing's related layer.


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to your project's **Drawings** tool.
  2. Click **number** next to the drawing you want to mark up. 
  3. Click the **Markup**![Markup In The Drawing Toolbar](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/690eed7f1b889b05c32e8c1fa29218f8/97849b368c8d5070d7b9f4577cc64966/markup-in-the-drawing-toolbar.jpg?fm=webp&h=41) icon
  4. Mark up your drawing or select the markup(s) that you want to edit by using the **Select** or **Multi Select** or Lasso markup tools. 


_Note_ : See [Mark Up a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/mark-up-a-drawing).
5. After selecting one or more markups, you can perform the following actions:
  * **Move** the markup(s) to a different location by dragging the item to a new location.
  * **Resize** the markup by dragging the corners of the markup to a new size and shape. _Note_ : This is only available if selecting one markup at a time.
  * **Click** the markup to access the following options:![2024 12 13 Markups Edit Actions Menu](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/414f6c67b5d271a70d05254da320c372/c47a3c9d91e31801f45a44dc8c7622d9/2024-12-13-markups-edit-actions-menu.png?fm=webp&h=24)* Click **Publish** to [publish the markup(s)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/publish-personal-drawing-markups).* Click the **copy** icon to save the markup(s) to your clipboard so you can paste it into a _different_ drawing.* Then navigate to another drawing, press **CONTROL / COMMAND** + **V** on your keyboard, and if the two drawings have the same dimensions, the markup will automatically paste to the same coordinates as the original drawing. You can move the markup's location as desired.* Once you publish the markup, collaborators can see it too. Learn more about [copying markups](https://support.procore.com/faq/what-is-the-difference-between-copy-and-duplicate-with-drawing-markups).* Click the **duplicate** icon to paste the markup(s) near the original one in the _same_ drawing. The duplicated markup is auto-selected so you can move it to the desired location within the drawing.* Once you publish the duplicated markup, collaborators can see it too. Learn more about [duplicating markups](https://support.procore.com/faq/what-is-the-difference-between-copy-and-duplicate-with-drawing-markups).* Click the **paperclip** ![Icon Link Item](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b232902e86447bbdd16b569625f86143/06d7a025357ad7eef896718f46ed98ab/icon-link-item.png?fm=webp&h=17) icon to [link related items](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/link-items-on-a-drawing) to the drawing. _Note_ : This is only available if the markup allows additional items to be linked.* Click the **trash bin** ![Icon Delete Trash](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/516e54ea0aa4258758d529172871e38f/b443eda53f8791b9421fb3aad5dee970/icon-delete-trash.png?fm=webp&h=18) icon to [delete the markup(s)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/delete-drawing-markups).


## See Also
  * [Allow Users To Delete Published Markups](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/allow-users-to-delete-published-markups)
  * [Publish Personal Drawing Markups](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/publish-personal-drawing-markups)
  * [What is the difference between personal and published drawing markups?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-difference-between-personal-and-published-drawing-markups)
title: Edit Drawing Markups
description: How to publish drawing markups using Procore's Drawings tool.
